goal: User wants to plan a new goal or project, but no specific goal details were provided.

steps.title: Define the Project Goal and Scope
steps.description: Clearly articulate what you want to build or achieve. Write a one-paragraph problem statement that describes the purpose, the target users, and the expected outcome. Avoid vague language — be as specific as possible.

steps.title: Research Existing Solutions and Technologies
steps.description: Investigate existing tools, libraries, frameworks, or products that already address your goal (fully or partially). Document their strengths and gaps to justify your approach. Choose your core tech stack based on your findings.

steps.title: Break Down Requirements into Features
steps.description: List all functional and non-functional requirements. Categorize them as Must-Have (MVP), Should-Have, and Nice-to-Have using the MoSCoW method. This keeps scope manageable and priorities clear.

steps.title: Design the System Architecture
steps.description: Sketch a high-level architecture diagram showing major components (e.g., frontend, backend, database, APIs, third-party services) and how they interact. Decide on data models, API contracts, or UI flows as appropriate.

steps.title: Set Up the Development Environment
steps.description: Initialize a version-controlled repository (e.g., GitHub/GitLab). Set up your chosen tech stack, configure linting/formatting tools, and establish a branching strategy (e.g., main/develop/feature branches).

steps.title: Build and Test the MVP (Minimum Viable Product)
steps.description: Implement only the Must-Have features identified in Step 3. Write unit and integration tests alongside the code. Focus on functionality over polish at this stage.

steps.title: Review, Gather Feedback, and Iterate
steps.description: Demo the MVP to stakeholders, teammates, or potential users. Collect structured feedback, log it as issues or tickets, and prioritize the next iteration based on impact and effort.

steps.title: Document the Project
steps.description: Write a README with setup instructions, usage examples, and architecture notes. Document APIs (e.g., using Swagger/OpenAPI) and add inline code comments where necessary.

steps.title: Plan Deployment and Maintenance Strategy
steps.description: Choose a deployment target (e.g., AWS, GCP, Vercel, Docker). Set up a CI/CD pipeline for automated testing and deployment. Define a maintenance plan including monitoring, logging, and update cadence.
